description: A fast edge rings and overshoots because an impedance discontinuity from a stub or width change reflects energy.
body: Failure mode: a controlled-impedance net hits a stub, abrupt width change, or unmatched connector, creating a reflection that appears as ringing on the edge. Detection signals: overshoot and ringing on a fast edge, reflections timed to a discontinuity location, and behavior that worsens at faster edge rates. Do not slow the driver as a permanent fix while the discontinuity remains. Safer fallback: remove the stub, keep the trace width constant, and add termination matched to the line impedance, then re-verify the edge.
